---
title: "Editorial typography on the web"
description: "Measure, leading, hierarchy: typographic fundamentals applied to a blog."
pubDate: 2026-06-20
tags: ["design", "typography"]
draft: false
---

Typography is 90% of a blog's design. The rest is restraint.

## Measure

A line of body text should hold between 60 and 75 characters. Beyond that, the eye gets lost on the way back to the next line; below it, reading becomes choppy.

## Leading

- Body text: 1.5 to 1.75
- Headings: 1.1 to 1.3
- Code: 1.6 minimum

> Good leading is invisible. Bad leading makes a text tiring without the reader ever knowing why.

## Hierarchy

1. A single h1 per page
2. Sequential levels, never jumping from h2 to h4
3. Weight and size create the hierarchy, not color

### Tabular figures

For dates and counters, tabular figures (`font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`) keep the layout from dancing around.

## Italics and emphasis

*Italics* signal nuance, **bold** signals importance. Both lose their power as soon as you overuse them, like `inline code` sprinkled everywhere.
